assertion_results.text: The verification report marks all 5 acceptance criteria as PASS — the diff implements single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 12: 'Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 5 of 5 criteria met'. The detailed table (lines 66-72) shows all 5 criteria as PASS: (1) Single license filter, (2) Multi license filter, (3) Invalid license returns 400, (4) Pagination integration, (5) Response shape unchanged. Each criterion file (criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md) also individually marks its verdict as PASS.

assertion_results.text: Scope containment is PASS — all files in the diff (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) are listed in the task's Files to Modify or Files to Create
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 7: 'Scope Containment | PASS | All 3 files match the task specification exactly'. The detailed Scope Containment section (lines 27-33) shows a file-by-file table: list.rs (Modify/Modified/MATCH), service/mod.rs (Modify/Modified/MATCH), tests/api/package.rs (Create/Created/MATCH). Line 35: 'No unexpected files were changed. No specified files are missing from the diff.'

assertion_results.text: No sub-tasks are created because there are no review comments (Review Feedback is N/A)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 5: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments on the PR'. report.md line 6: 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created'. There is no mention anywhere in the outputs of sub-tasks being created.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only report files: report.md, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md. These are all verification/reporting artifacts. No code files (e.g., .rs, .py, .js) appear in the outputs. The report itself only references reading and analyzing diff content — no mention of modifying, editing, or writing to any source code files.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md concludes with 'Overall: PASS' and 'The PR correctly implements the license filter feature... All acceptance criteria are satisfied with code-level evidence from the diff.' There is no mention of merging, approving, or any automated merge action. The report presents findings (pass/fail verdicts with evidence) for human review.

assertion_results.text: Sensitive pattern scan is PASS — the diff contains no passwords, API keys, or private keys
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 10: 'Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No passwords, API keys, tokens, .env files, or hardcoded credentials detected'. The detailed Security section (lines 50-57) lists each category scanned: hardcoded passwords/secrets (None), API keys/tokens (None), private keys/certificates (None), .env file changes (None), credential/connection strings (None).

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/package.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: 'Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | New test file created; no existing tests modified or removed'. The detailed Style/Conventions section (lines 94-96) elaborates: 'A new test file tests/api/package.rs was created with 4 integration tests. No existing test files were modified, removed, or weakened. All changes are purely additive.'

assertion_results.text: The verification report assembles verdicts from all four domain sub-agents: Scope Containment, Diff Size, and Commit Traceability from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns from Security; CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands from Correctness; Test Quality (combining Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, and Eval Quality) and Test Change Classification from Style/Conventions
assertion_results.evidence: report.md summary table (lines 5-15) contains all required rows: Review Feedback, Root-Cause Investigation, Scope Containment, Diff Size, Commit Traceability, Sensitive Patterns, CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, Test Quality, Test Change Classification, Verification Commands. The detailed sections are organized by domain: Intent Alignment (lines 23-43: Scope Containment, Diff Size, Commit Traceability), Security (lines 47-57: Sensitive Patterns), Correctness (lines 61-82: Acceptance Criteria, CI Status, Verification Commands), Style/Conventions (lines 86-96: Test Quality with sub-components Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, Eval Quality; Test Change Classification).

assertion_results.text: The report includes detailed findings with specific evidence for each domain — file-by-file scope comparison (Intent Alignment), line-level pattern scanning results (Security), per-criterion code-level verification (Correctness), and test quality assessment (Style/Conventions) — not just pass/fail verdicts in the summary table
assertion_results.evidence: Intent Alignment: Lines 27-35 contain a file-by-file scope comparison table (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs with Task Spec/Diff/Status columns). Security: Lines 51-56 list each scanned pattern category with results. Correctness: Lines 66-72 provide a per-criterion table with Key Evidence column citing specific code constructs (e.g., 'validate_license_param parses single value', 'Condition::any() with is_in gives union semantics'). Additionally, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md provide multi-paragraph code-level analysis per criterion. Style/Conventions: Lines 90-92 assess Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, and Eval Quality individually.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — the 3-criteria detection (author github-actions[bot], marker ## Eval Results, footer sdlc-workflow/run-evals) found no matches, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 92: 'Eval Quality: N/A -- no eval result reviews exist on this PR (no reviews from github-actions[bot] with "## Eval Results" marker and "sdlc-workflow/run-evals" footer).' This explicitly states all three detection criteria (author github-actions[bot], marker ## Eval Results, footer sdlc-workflow/run-evals) and confirms N/A status. Test Quality overall is PASS (line 13), meaning Eval Quality's N/A status did not negatively affect the combination.

assertion_results.text: No eval failure sub-tasks are created because Eval Quality is N/A — eval failure sub-tasks are only created when Eval Quality is WARN (at least one eval assertion failed)
assertion_results.evidence: Eval Quality is N/A (report.md line 92). No mention of eval failure sub-tasks anywhere in the output files. report.md line 6: 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created'. No sub-tasks of any kind were created.

assertion_results.text: Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation verdicts are determined by the orchestrator independently from domain analysis — Review Feedback is N/A because no review comments exist, Root-Cause Investigation is N/A because no sub-tasks were created
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 5-6: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments on the PR' and 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created'. These appear as top-level rows in the summary table (lines 5-6) before the domain-specific rows (Scope Containment on line 7 onwards), indicating they are orchestrator-level verdicts separate from the four domain sections (Intent Alignment, Security, Correctness, Style/Conventions) that follow.
